content.problemStatement: Write a C program that takes an integer as input and determines whether it is an even or odd number. Print "Even" if the number is even and "Odd" if it is odd.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "Even" if N is even, otherwise print "Odd". Each answer should be on its own line.

solution.approach: The parity of an integer is determined by its remainder when divided by 2. If N % 2 equals 0 the number is even, otherwise it is odd. The algorithm simply reads T, then for each test case reads N, computes N % 2 and prints the corresponding word. This runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space, which easily satisfies the limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){int N; if(scanf("%d",&N)!=1) break; if(N%2==0) printf("Even\n"); else printf("Odd\n"); } return 0; }
code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t=int(data[0])
    out=[]
    idx=1
    for _ in range(t):
        n=int(data[idx]); idx+=1
        out.append('Even' if n%2==0 else 'Odd')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__=='__main__':
    main()
